name: Barmah Park Vineyard
signature: A rustic, intimate cellar door offering a five-wine, five-canape pairing experience from a hatted kitchen — the signature tasting format on the Peninsula
editorNote:
Barmah Park has quietly turned itself into the Peninsula's most serious cellar-door-as-dining-experience. The signature tasting is a structured five-wine, five-canape pairing run out of a small kitchen that cooks like a hatted restaurant: one canape designed for each wine, paced and explained, across a single relaxed hour at a communal wooden table. It is the only cellar door on the Peninsula offering this format as its default.

The wines are quietly excellent — Italian-leaning varieties alongside the Peninsula standards — but the real draw is the format. Most Peninsula tasting rooms run on parallel counters and brisk pours; Barmah Park sits you down, feeds you, and turns a flight into a small meal. For anyone who finds the bigger estates impersonal, this is the corrective.

Book ahead — the pairing experience runs limited sessions. Weekday mornings are the best way to have the place almost to yourself.
